title: Dynamic Maps
text: add_species_data() fetches OBIS occurrence tiles taxon adds colour-scaled fill layer map. Default species (taxonid 126436) blank map: Custom species custom breaks legend: Fit map view species extent adding layer:
code: blank_map() |> add_species_data() grey_map() |>     add_species_data(         taxonid = 955271,         breaks  = c(0, 5, 50, 500, 5000),         legend  = TRUE     ) ext <- get_species_extent(taxonid = 955271)  blank_map(projection = "mercator") |>     add_species_data(taxonid = 955271) |>     fit_bounds(ext)

title: Dynamic Maps
text: Pass named OBIS palette via palette argument add_species_data() call obis_pal() internally generate one colour per break. works palettes scale_fill_obis_cont() scale_color_obis_cat(). Pair fewer breaks diverging palette: obis_pal() can also called directly preview colours pass mapgl layer functions:
code: grey_map() |> add_species_data(taxonid = 126436, palette = "thermal") grey_map() |> add_species_data(taxonid = 126436, palette = "blues") blank_map() |>     add_species_data(         taxonid = 126436,         breaks  = c(0, 10, 100, 1000),         palette = "rdbu",         legend  = TRUE     ) obis_pal("thermal", 5) #> [1] "#042333" "#5580B8" "#CDE6C9" "#F3BA3B" "#B12010" #> [1] "#042333" "#4a69a9" "#cde6c9" "#f0a120" "#b12010"  obis_pal("rdbu", 7) #> [1] "#053061" "#3783BB" "#A6CFE3" "#F7F7F7" "#F7B799" "#CA4841" "#67001F" obis_pal("blues", 4, direction = -1)  # reversed: dark → light #> [1] "#08306B" "#3786C0" "#ABCFE5" "#F7FBFF"

title: Dynamic Maps
text: add_map_controls() adds fullscreen, globe-projection toggle, scale bar controls map. Disable globe toggle move scale bar:
code: blank_map(projection = "none") |> add_map_controls() grey_map() |>     add_map_controls(globe = FALSE, scale_position = "bottom-right")

what: Inline H3J source (requires duckdb, h3jsr, dplyr)
title: Dynamic Maps
text: add_h3j_source_inline() encodes data frame H3 cells data: URI registers vector source — file tile server needed. Use mapgl layer function (e.g. mapgl::add_fill_layer()).
code: library(duckdb) #> Loading required package: DBI library(h3jsr) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  con <- dbConnect(duckdb())  dbSendQuery(con, "install httpfs; load httpfs;") #> <duckdb_result d6d30 connection=bf2f0 statement='install httpfs; load httpfs;'>  df <- dbGetQuery(con, "     SELECT records, cell     FROM read_parquet('s3://obis-products/speciesgrids/h3_7/*')     WHERE species = 'Minuca rapax' ")  df <- df |>     mutate(h3 = h3jsr::get_parent(cell, 3)) |>     group_by(h3) |>     summarise(value = sum(records))  blank_map(projection = "mercator") |>     add_h3j_source_inline("h3source", df = df, h3_col = "h3") |>     mapgl::add_fill_layer(         id           = "h3layer",         source       = "h3source",         fill_color   = mapgl::interpolate(             column = "value",             values = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),             stops  = c("#2c7bb6", "#abd9e9", "#ffffbf", "#fdae61", "#d7191c")         ),         fill_opacity = 0.8     ) |>     mapgl::fit_bounds(get_species_extent(taxonid = 955271))

title: Dynamic Maps
text: maplibre_h3() creates self-contained htmlwidget combining MapLibre GL basemap DeckGL H3HexagonLayer. Suitable species richness, record counts, per-H3-cell metric. Enable 3-D extrusion extruded = TRUE:
code: # df from the duckdb query above maplibre_h3(df, center = c(-50, -10), zoom = 2) maplibre_h3(df, center = c(-50, -10), zoom = 2, extruded = TRUE, elevation_scale = 1000)

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: theme_obis() provides clean white background subtle blue-grey accents.  Axis titles placed far end axis default (corner_axis_titles = TRUE). Set FALSE centered titles:  Faceted plots legend placement also work box:   Use base_size scale everything presentations:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 2) +     labs(title = "Fuel efficiency", subtitle = "Weight vs. miles per gallon",          x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "MPG") +     theme_obis() ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 2) +     labs(title = "Fuel efficiency", subtitle = "Weight vs. miles per gallon",          x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "MPG") +     theme_obis(corner_axis_titles = FALSE) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 2) +     facet_wrap(~cyl) +     labs(title = "Fuel efficiency by cylinder count") +     theme_obis() ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length, color = Species)) +     geom_point(size = 2) +     labs(title = "Iris measurements") +     theme_obis(legend_position = "bottom") ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(color = "#009E73", size = 2.5) +     labs(title = "Presentation-ready", x = "Weight", y = "MPG") +     theme_obis(base_size = 14)

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: theme_obis_dark() uses deep navy background pairs well "mako", "thermal", "haline" continuous scales.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(color = "#56B4E9", size = 2) +     labs(title = "Deep ocean style", subtitle = "Weight vs. miles per gallon",          x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "MPG") +     theme_obis_dark() ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     labs(title = "Old Faithful eruption density") +     scale_fill_obis_cont("thermal") +     theme_obis_dark(legend_position = "bottom") ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length, color = Species)) +     geom_point(size = 2) +     facet_wrap(~Species) +     labs(title = "Iris by species") +     scale_color_obis_cat() +     theme_obis_dark()

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: theme_obis_pub() follows visual conventions top scientific journals (Nature, Science, Cell, One Earth). Key characteristics: L-shaped axes — bottom left axis lines , box, theme_classic(). Outward tick marks — black, short (3 pt), grid default. legend box — minimal legend tight spacing. Blank facet strips — bold label panel, coloured background. Compact margins — reduces whitespace around plot area. default base_size 11, comfortable screen preview standard figure dimensions (7 × 5 ). Reduce 7–8 pt narrow journal column widths, let obis_ggsave() handle scaling automatically.  Combine colorblind-safe categorical scale legend:  Subtle y-axis reference lines can enabled grid = "y" plots reading precise values matters:  Facets follow blank-strip convention used many journals (panel labels typically added later via cowplot patchwork):
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(size = 1.5) +     labs(title = "Fuel efficiency",          x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "MPG") +     theme_obis_pub() ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length, color = Species)) +     geom_point(size = 1.5) +     scale_color_obis_cat() +     labs(title = "Iris measurements",          x = "Sepal length (cm)", y = "Petal length (cm)") +     theme_obis_pub(legend_position = "bottom") ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(size = 1.5) +     labs(title = "With reference lines", x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "MPG") +     theme_obis_pub(grid = "y") ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(size = 1.5) +     facet_wrap(~cyl) +     labs(title = "Fuel efficiency by cylinder count",          x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "MPG") +     theme_obis_pub() ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("haline") +     guides(fill = guide_colourbar_h(title = "Density")) +     labs(x = "Waiting time (min)", y = "Eruption duration (min)") +     theme_obis_pub(legend_position = "bottom")

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: common frustration ggsave() text theme elements specified absolute points, appear proportionally smaller save figure larger physical size. obis_ggsave() fixes rendering plot reference size (default 7 × 5 , matching ggplot2’s screen default) scaling canvas target dimensions. Text always occupies fraction figure. scaling factor geometric mean area ratio: s=(w×h)/(wref×href)s = \sqrt{(w \times h)\;/\;(w_\text{ref} \times h_\text{ref})}. figures authored non-default preview size, adjust ref_width ref_height accordingly:
code: p_pub <- ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length, color = Species)) +     geom_point(size = 1.5) +     scale_color_obis_cat() +     labs(title = "Iris measurements",          x = "Sepal length (cm)", y = "Petal length (cm)") +     theme_obis_pub()  # Single-column journal width (3.5 × 2.5 in) — text scales down proportionally obis_ggsave("fig1_single.pdf", p_pub, width = 3.5, height = 2.5)  # Double-column (7 × 5 in) — same apparent text size as on screen obis_ggsave("fig1_double.pdf", p_pub, width = 7, height = 5)  # Poster panel (14 × 10 in) — text scales up, remains legible obis_ggsave("fig1_poster.pdf", p_pub, width = 14, height = 10)  # PNG for journal submission at 300 dpi obis_ggsave("fig1.png", p_pub, width = 3.5, height = 2.5, dpi = 300) # Plot designed for a 5 × 3.5 in RStudio preview obis_ggsave("fig1.pdf", p_pub, width = 3.5, height = 2.5,             ref_width = 5, ref_height = 3.5)

text: IBM’s 5-color palette (blue, purple, magenta, orange, gold) good choice number categories ≤ 5 cooler, corporate look preferred. works well light dark backgrounds.
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length, color = Species)) +     geom_point(size = 2.5) +     scale_color_obis_cat(palette = "ibm") +     labs(title = "IBM palette", color = "Species") +     theme_obis() ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length, color = Species)) +     geom_point(size = 2.5) +     scale_color_obis_cat(palette = "ibm") +     labs(title = "IBM palette — dark theme", color = "Species") +     theme_obis_dark()

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: scale_fill_obis_cont() scale_color_obis_cont() offer nine palettes — five multi-hue sequential, two diverging, two single-hue sequential:
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("mako") +     labs(title = "Mako — density", x = "Waiting (min)", y = "Eruption (min)") +     theme_obis() ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("thermal") +     labs(title = "Thermal — SST style") +     theme_obis_dark() ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("haline") +     labs(title = "Haline — salinity style") +     theme_obis() ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("deep", direction = -1) +     labs(title = "Deep — bathymetry style (reversed)") +     theme_obis()

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: Diverging palettes suited data meaningful midpoint (zero, reference temperature, species mean, etc.). 11-stop ColorBrewer palettes white neutral centre.
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("rdbu") +     labs(title = "RdBu — blue to red", x = "Waiting (min)", y = "Eruption (min)") +     theme_obis() ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("prgn") +     labs(title = "PRGn — purple to green", x = "Waiting (min)", y = "Eruption (min)") +     theme_obis()

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: "blues" "greens" 9-stop ColorBrewer palettes running near- white tint rich dark hue. work well whenever single variable needs highlighted without introducing hue contrast — example, highlighting species richness chlorophyll concentration neutral basemap.   Use direction = -1 reverse palette. scales also work color aesthetic scatter plots:
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("blues") +     labs(title = "Blues", x = "Waiting (min)", y = "Eruption (min)") +     theme_obis() ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("greens") +     labs(title = "Greens", x = "Waiting (min)", y = "Eruption (min)") +     theme_obis() ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = hp)) +     geom_point(size = 3) +     scale_color_obis_cont("haline") +     labs(title = "Continuous color on points", color = "Horsepower") +     theme_obis()

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: guide_colourbar_h() returns thin horizontal colourbar styled similarly matplotlib’s default. Use guides() placing legend bottom.  Adjust width title position needed:
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("thermal") +     guides(fill = guide_colourbar_h(title = "Density")) +     labs(title = "Horizontal colourbar", x = "Waiting (min)", y = "Eruption (min)") +     theme_obis(legend_position = "bottom") ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("haline") +     guides(fill = guide_colourbar_h(         title          = "Density",         barwidth       = grid::unit(16, "lines"),         title_position = "bottom",         title_hjust    = 0     )) +     labs(title = "Wider bar, bottom title") +     theme_obis(legend_position = "bottom") ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("mako") +     guides(fill = guide_colourbar_h(title = "Density")) +     labs(title = "Dark theme with colourbar") +     theme_obis_dark(legend_position = "bottom")

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: obis_add_logo() overlays OBIS IOC logos corner finished plot using cowplot. Pass fig_asp = width / height match dimensions plan use ggsave().     fig_asp argument corrects logo proportions match specific output size (e.g. 8 × 6 inches):  Logos also work dark theme plots:
code: p_light <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +     geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 2) +     labs(title = "Fuel efficiency", subtitle = "Weight vs. miles per gallon",          x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "MPG") +     theme_obis()  obis_add_logo(p_light) obis_add_logo(p_light, position = "bottomleft") obis_add_logo(p_light, position = "topright") obis_add_logo(p_light, logo_size = 0.10) obis_add_logo(p_light, fig_asp = 8 / 6) p_dark <- ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("thermal") +     guides(fill = guide_colourbar_h(title = "Density")) +     labs(title = "Old Faithful eruption density",          x = "Waiting time (min)", y = "Eruption duration (min)") +     theme_obis_dark(legend_position = "bottom")  obis_add_logo(p_dark)

title: ggplot2 Styles and Themes
text: combined plot mixing categorical shape continuous color scales horizontal colourbar:  full dark marine look title, subtitle, caption:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = hp, shape = factor(cyl))) +     geom_point(size = 3) +     scale_color_obis_cont("mako") +     scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 18)) +     guides(color = guide_colourbar_h(title = "Horsepower",                                      barwidth = grid::unit(8, "lines"))) +     labs(title = "Combined scales", x = "Weight", y = "MPG",          shape = "Cylinders") +     theme_obis(legend_position = "bottom") ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_obis_cont("thermal") +     guides(fill = guide_colourbar_h(title = "Density")) +     labs(         title    = "Old Faithful eruption patterns",         subtitle = "Kernel density estimate",         caption  = "Source: R datasets",         x = "Waiting time (min)", y = "Eruption duration (min)"     ) +     theme_obis_dark(legend_position = "bottom")

text: prepare_grid_data() bins numeric records column ordered factor levels matching OBIS colour breaks, ready scale_fill_manual(). Custom break thresholds:

title: Static Maps
text: get_occ_gridded() retrieves raw occurrence records via OBIS API converts spatial grid. get_occ_tiles() uses pre-aggregated tile endpoint — much faster large taxa.
code: # Whale shark (taxonid 141438) grid_geohash <- get_occ_gridded(taxonid = 141438, type_grid = "geohash", grid_res = 3) grid_h3      <- get_occ_gridded(taxonid = 141438, type_grid = "h3",      grid_res = 3) grid_a5      <- get_occ_gridded(taxonid = 141438, type_grid = "a5",      grid_res = 3)  # With date filter grid_recent <- get_occ_gridded(     taxonid   = 141438,     startdate = "2010-01-01",     enddate   = "2024-12-31",     type_grid = "geohash",     grid_res  = 3 ) tiles       <- get_occ_tiles(taxonid = 141438, grid_res = 3) tiles_r4    <- get_occ_tiles(taxonid = 141438, grid_res = 4) tiles_dated <- get_occ_tiles(     taxonid   = 141438,     startdate = "2020-01-01",     enddate   = "2024-12-31",     grid_res  = 3 )

title: Static Maps
text: add_species_data_s() overlays spatial grid existing basemap. default zooms bounding box data. Pass auto_breaks = TRUE let ggplot2 choose break thresholds automatically: Global view without zooming data extent: Custom breaks, colours, legend: Regional view — Indo-Pacific:
code: blank_map_s() |>     add_species_data_s(grid_data = grid_geohash) grey_map_s() |>     add_species_data_s(         grid_data   = grid_h3,         auto_breaks = TRUE     ) grey_map_s() |>     add_species_data_s(         grid_data     = tiles,         limit_by_bbox = FALSE     ) blank_map_s() |>     add_species_data_s(         grid_data = tiles,         breaks    = c(1, 5, 50, 500),         colors    = c("#f7fbff", "#6baed6", "#2171b5", "#084594"),         legend    = FALSE     ) grey_map_s(plot_xlim = c(30, 160), plot_ylim = c(-40, 35)) |>     add_species_data_s(         grid_data     = tiles,         limit_by_bbox = FALSE,         plot_xlim     = c(30, 160),         plot_ylim     = c(-40, 35)     )

what: Visualization and Mapping Tools for OBIS Data
title: Visualization and Mapping Tools for OBIS Data
text: Internal use . package developed OBIS Secretariat OBIS nodes. API may change without notice internal workflows evolve. obisrecipes R package reusable visualization mapping functions Ocean Biodiversity Information System (OBIS) data. covers three areas: ggplot2 styles — light theme, dark marine theme, colorblind-safe color scales, horizontal colourbar guide, OBIS/IOC logo overlay. Static maps — ggplot2-based world basemaps, grid converters (geohash, H3, A5), occurrence layer plotting species distributions. Dynamic maps — interactive WebGL maps via mapgl, DeckGL H3 hexagon widget, helpers adding species data map controls.

code: library(obisrecipes) library(ggplot2) library(robis) suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr)) obis_load_fonts() # Call once per session  occ_acch <- occurrence(taxonid = 159580, startdate = "2010-01-01") #>  Retrieved 5000 records of approximately 20018 (24%) Retrieved 10000 records of #> approximately 20018 (49%) Retrieved 15000 records of approximately 20018 #> (74%) Retrieved 20000 records of approximately 20018 (99%) Retrieved 20018 #> records of approximately 20018 (100%)  ggplot(occ_acch, aes(sst, depth)) +     geom_point(size = 2.5, color = "#188eb6", alpha = .2) +     scale_color_obis_cat() +     labs(title = "Acanthurus chirurgus",          subtitle = "doctorfish",          x = "SST", y = "Depth (in m)") +     theme_obis(legend_position = "bottom") #> Warning: Removed 881 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). occ_acan <- occurrence(     taxonid = 125515, startdate = "2015-01-01",     geometry = "POLYGON ((-81.5625 31.653381, -97.910156 30.448674,                         -99.492188 21.125498, -92.460937 16.636192,                          -83.847656 11.005904, -74.707031 7.188101,                           -58.710938 7.536764, -52.910156 13.068777,                            -61.171875 28.767659, -73.125 34.016242,                             -81.5625 31.653381))" ) #>  Retrieved 5000 records of approximately 38569 (12%) Retrieved 10000 records of #> approximately 38569 (25%) Retrieved 15000 records of approximately 38569 #> (38%) Retrieved 20000 records of approximately 38569 (51%) Retrieved 25000 #> records of approximately 38569 (64%) Retrieved 30000 records of approximately #> 38569 (77%) Retrieved 35000 records of approximately 38569 (90%) Retrieved 38569 #> records of approximately 38569 (100%)  occ_acan_agg <- occ_acan |>     group_by(date_year, species) |>     summarise(Records = n()) |>     rename(Year = date_year, Species = species) |>     filter(!is.na(Species)) #> `summarise()` has regrouped the output. #> ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by date_year and species. #> ℹ Output is grouped by date_year. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(date_year, species))` for per-operation grouping #>   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.  ggplot(occ_acan_agg, aes(Year, Records, fill = Species)) +     geom_bar(stat = "identity", position="dodge") +     scale_fill_obis_cat() +     labs(title = "Acanthuridae",          subtitle = "surgeonfishes",          caption = "Limited to a region in the Caribbean and starting in 2010",          x = "Year", y = "Records") +     theme_obis(legend_position = "bottom")

title: Visualization and Mapping Tools for OBIS Data
text: Dynamic maps require internet connection render interactive widgets. Run interactively RStudio embed R Markdown / Quarto documents:   Using H3 grid (first example uses h3j source mapgl package, suitable large datasets. larger datasets uses H3 DeckGL widget, second third examples).
code: grey_map() |>     add_species_data(taxonid = 126436, legend = TRUE) grey_map(projection = "mercator") |>     add_species_data(taxonid = 126436, legend = TRUE) library(duckdb) library(h3jsr) library(dplyr)  con <- dbConnect(duckdb())  df <- dbGetQuery(con, "     SELECT records, cell     FROM read_parquet('s3://obis-products/speciesgrids/h3_7/*')     WHERE species = 'Minuca rapax' ")  df <- df |>     mutate(h3 = h3jsr::get_parent(cell, 3)) |>     group_by(h3) |>     summarise(value = sum(records))  # inline H3 blank_map(projection = "mercator") |>     add_h3j_source_inline("h3source", df = df, h3_col = "h3") |>     mapgl::add_fill_layer(         id           = "h3layer",         source       = "h3source",         fill_color   = mapgl::interpolate(             column = "value",             values = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),             stops  = c("#2c7bb6", "#abd9e9", "#ffffbf", "#fdae61", "#d7191c")         ),         fill_opacity = 0.8     ) |>     mapgl::fit_bounds(get_species_extent(taxonid = 955271))  # DeckGL maplibre_h3(df, center = c(-50, -10), zoom = 2)  # DeckGL with extrusion maplibre_h3(df, center = c(-50, -10), extruded = TRUE, elevation_scale = 3000)

code: add_species_data(   map,   taxonid = 126436,   startdate = NULL,   enddate = NULL,   breaks = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000),   colors = c("#2c7bb6", "#abd9e9", "#ffffbf", "#fdae61", "#d7191c"),   palette = NULL,   legend = FALSE )

title: Add OBIS species occurrence data to a map — add_species_data
text: map map object returned blank_map(), grey_map(), carto_map(), compatible maplibre/mapboxgl object. taxonid Integer character. OBIS taxon identifier. Default: 126436. startdate Character NULL. ISO 8601 start date filter (e.g. "2000-01-01"). Default: NULL (filter). enddate Character NULL. ISO 8601 end date filter. Default: NULL. breaks Numeric vector. Breakpoints colour scale. Default: c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000). colors Character vector CSS colours, one per break. Ignored palette set. Default: blue--red diverging palette. palette Character NULL. Name OBIS palette passed obis_pal() generate length(breaks) colours automatically. Accepts name supported scale_fill_obis_cont() (e.g. "thermal", "rdbu", "blues") scale_color_obis_cat() (e.g. "okabe_ito"). NULL (default) colors argument used -. legend Logical. Whether add categorical legend. Default: FALSE.

TRUE, zooms map bounding box grid_data. Default TRUE. plot_xlim Numeric vector length 2, NULL. Manual longitude limits used limit_by_bbox = FALSE. Default NULL. plot_ylim Numeric vector length 2, NULL. Manual latitude limits used limit_by_bbox = FALSE. Default NULL. map_crs Numeric character CRS, NA WGS 84. Default NA. auto_breaks Logical. TRUE, uses ggplot2::scale_fill_binned() automatic breaks instead obis_scale(). Default FALSE. breaks Numeric vector bin boundaries passed obis_scale() prepare_grid_data(). Default c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000). colors Character vector fill colors, one per bin. Default OBIS blue--red palette. legend Logical. Show fill legend. Default TRUE. ... what: obisrecipes: Visualization and mapping tools for OBIS data — obisrecipes-package
title: obisrecipes: Visualization and mapping tools for OBIS data — obisrecipes-package
text: obisrecipes provides curated set reusable functions visualizing mapping biodiversity data Ocean Biodiversity Information System (OBIS). developed primarily internal use OBIS Secretariat OBIS nodes, covers three main areas: ggplot2 styles — light theme (theme_obis()), dark marine theme (theme_obis_dark()), colorblind-safe categorical continuous color scales, horizontal colourbar guide, logo overlay helper. Static maps — ggplot2-based world basemaps (blank_map_s(), grey_map_s()), grid converters (geohash, H3, A5), occurrence layer function plotting species distributions. Dynamic maps — interactive WebGL maps via mapgl (blank_map(), grey_map(), carto_map()), DeckGL H3 hexagon widget (maplibre_h3()), helpers adding species data map controls.

title: obisrecipes: Visualization and mapping tools for OBIS data — obisrecipes-package
text: package designed OBIS Secretariat staff OBIS node managers produce maps data-visualization outputs using OBIS data. API may change without notice internal workflows evolve.

title: Colorblind-safe categorical color scale — scale_color_obis_cat
text: Applies discrete, colorblind-safe palette color aesthetic. Three palettes available: "okabe_ito" — Okabe-Ito 8-color palette (default), widely recommended colorblind accessibility. "tol" — Paul Tol's 7-color bright scheme, good alternative. "ibm" — IBM's 5-color colorblind-safe palette (blue, purple, magenta, orange, gold), designed data visualization light backgrounds.

what: Publication-quality ggplot2 theme for OBIS — theme_obis_pub
title: Publication-quality ggplot2 theme for OBIS — theme_obis_pub
text: minimal, journal-ready theme following visual conventions top scientific publications (Nature, Science, Cell, One Earth). Key characteristics: clean L-shaped axes outward tick marks, background grid, compact layout suited single- multi-panel figures. Call obis_load_fonts() per session enable Roboto font.

title: Publication-quality ggplot2 theme for OBIS — theme_obis_pub
code: theme_obis_pub(   base_size = 11,   base_family = "roboto",   legend_position = "right",   grid = c("none", "y", "xy") )

title: Publication-quality ggplot2 theme for OBIS — theme_obis_pub
text: base_size Numeric. Base font size pt. Default 11. Reduce 7–8 pt exporting narrow journal column widths (≤ 3.5 ), use obis_ggsave() handle scaling automatically. base_family Character. Base font family. Default "roboto". Swap "helvetica" "arial" available system. legend_position Character "none". Position legend. Default "right". grid Character. Reference lines draw: "none" (default, typical Nature / Science), "y" subtle major y-axis lines , "xy" axes.

title: Publication-quality ggplot2 theme for OBIS — theme_obis_pub
text: ggplot2::theme object.

title: Publication-quality ggplot2 theme for OBIS — theme_obis_pub
text: default base_size 11 comfortable screen use figures saved standard dimensions (≈ 7 × 5 ). targeting narrow column widths print journals (e.g. 3.5 single-column), either reduce base_size 7–8 use obis_ggsave(), rescales elements proportionally output dimensions.
